items.title: Watch: Shashi Tharoor column: Terror attacks may have dropped, but the world is becoming ‘unevenly unsafe’
items.description: At first glance, the macroeconomic indicators of global security offer a rare moment of statistical comfort. Recent reports suggest that global terrorism experienced a noteworthy decline in 2025, with fatalities around the world falling to 5,582 across 2,944 recorded incidents — representing a 28% drop in deaths and a 22% reduction in overall attacks. With as many as 81 nations registering measurable improvements in their domestic security landscapes, a superficial reading of the data might suggest that the international community is finally turning the tide against terror, or as the strategists prefer to call it, asymmetric warfare. Yet, there lies a far more unsettling reality: the world is not necessarily becoming safer; it is becoming unevenly unsafe.

items.title: 'This is our sky': Palestinians fly kites in defiance of Israeli settlers in West Bank
items.description: Every summer since 2009, residents of a Palestinian village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank have gathered for a kite festival. The event is held on land that they say has been partly lost since a settlement was established in 1983. The festival, while primarily for children, carries a "political message", according to one of its organisers.

items.title: Meet the crypto billionaires building a world where money buys you a vote
items.description: They think democracy has run its course - so they're setting up their own nations instead.

items.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
items.description: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

items.title: Milei urges governors to renew commitment to 'May Pact' in speech
items.description: President says Argentina is entering a "Second Independence" as he outlines new reform agenda and praises his "cabinet of patriots." Leer más

items.title: France ends Morocco’s run at World Cup 2026
items.description: Morocco’s historic World Cup 2026 journey came to an end as France secured a 2–0 victory in the quarter-finals, bringing the African team’s impressive campaign to a close.

items.title: Milei’s allied governors open door to suspension of PASO primaries
items.description: Government is seeking support to advance with its electoral reform push ahead of next voting cycle; Senate to have final say on potential suspension of compulsory primaries. Leer más

items.title: Could AI help al-Qaeda and other groups plan terror attacks?
items.description: Followers of extremist groups regularly ask how AI can help them plan terrorist attacks. A new study suggests that about one-third of AI chatbots might help them, if asked the right way.

items.title: Argentina's gruelling World Cup schedule a concern for Scaloni
items.description: Leandro Paredes, Nicolás Tagliafico and Julián Álvarez set to return to starting line-up for clash against Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Japan’s JAXA successfully launches, lands reusable rocket
items.description: The prototype, launched from the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency's test site in Noshiro, Akita region, reached a height of about 10 metres (33 feet), and then landed

items.title: SK Hynix shares jump 14% in Nasdaq debut
US Rep Ro Khanna speaks out about being harassed by Israeli settlers
items.description: US Rep Ro Khanna speaks out about being harassed by Israeli settlers

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
items.description: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Live: Bulldogs
items.description: Western Bulldogs and West Coast kick off a Sunday triple-header as round 18 of the AFL comes to a close. Follow live.
items.source: Eagles kicks off Sunday footy at Docklands

items.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
items.description: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

items.title: Evacuations as landslide threatens Indian village, construction firm blamed
items.description: A landslide during heavy monsoon rains tore through the Indian hill town of Shimla, forcing villagers to evacuate

items.title: At least 88 terrorists killed in ongoing anti-terror operation in Pakistan’s Balochistan
items.description: The military, paramilitary Rangers and the Frontier Corps are participating in the ongoing operation, which has involved both ground and air offensives against the militants

items.title: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
items.description: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

items.title: Amnesty calls latest US third-country deportation to Eswatini unlawful
items.description: Eleven more migrants arrived in Mbabane on Wednesday as part of President Donald Trump's wide-ranging immigration crackdown.

items.title: Global population at 8.3 billion: What does that mean for anyone under 30?
items.description: With the world population surpassing 8.3 billion today, can we truly realize the hopes and dreams of the largest generation of young people ever? On World Population Day 2026, the spotlight is on empowering youth to shape their futures through education, health, and opportunity. From family planning to sustainable development, this global observance asks a vital question: are we investing enough in tomorrow's leaders today? Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Diene Keita, Executive Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

items.title: At Canada's biggest rodeo, the starting gun is fired in the fight over Alberta separation
items.description: Canadian unity takes centre stage at the Calgary Stampede as fears of a Brexit-style upset looms over the October vote on Alberta's future.

items.title: U.S. Democrat Ro Khanna detained briefly by Israeli settlers in Palestine
items.description: Mr. Khanna, a Democrat from California, was visiting the ruins of Khirbet Zanuta, a Bedouin village in the southern West Bank that was abandoned after attacks from Israeli settlers when the incident took place

items.title: Publicly declare Strait of Hormuz open, won't attack ships: U.S. demand to Iran
items.description: U.S. officials said that Donald Trump is giving U.S. negotiators limited time to reach a deal with Iran, but, in a sign of the challenges ahead, they underscored that the President had a wide range of options if talks fall apart

items.title: 11 wounded in overnight Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
items.description: At least 11 people, including a child, were wounded after Russia launched an overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv early Saturday, according to Ukraine’s State Emergency Service.

items.title: Nvidia supplier King Yuan Electronics to invest up to $1.4 billion in US facility
Central Bank survey sees inflation dipping below 2% from August
items.description: REM market expectations survey of consultancy firms, economists and experts forecasts 2026 annual inflation of 30%, with peso trading at 1,673 per US dollar by year-end. Leer más

items.title: New $900 million pledge to expand clean cooking in Africa
items.description: African nations have secured 900 million dollars in new funding to expand access to clean cooking technologies, bringing total commitments to more than 3.1 billion dollars since last year's Africa Clean Cooking Summit.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Luis Caputo: Argentina has secured funds for 2026 debt payments
items.description: Economy Minister Luis Caputo says Milei government has secured enough funds to meet this year's external debt obligations; Administration open to international markets to refinance future maturities, but not in a rush. Leer más

items.title: S.Africa minister raises concerns about security at Zimbabwe border
items.description: "Something has to be done urgently" about border security at the Limpopo river border with Zimbabwe, says South Africa's minister of Public works and Infrastructure, Dean Macpherson after visiting the area. "It was shocking...there literally is no border in some places," he says.

items.title: At least 15 Indian tourists dead after boat capsizes near island off Vietnam; rescue efforts under way
items.description: For any assistance, the first control room can be reached at: +84 36 281 7930, +84 91 552 37 14 and +84 33 452 0414; the other one in Hanoi can be reached at: +84 91 308 9165

items.title: The man solving the '100-million-year-old cold case' death of a dinosaur
items.description: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

items.title: SpaceX's near-term AI payoff seen tethered to Earth, not outer space
Mojtaba vows revenge for father’s killing after Trump’s new threat
items.description: ‘Vengeance is the will of our nation and must inevitably be carried out,’ says Iran’s Supreme Leader in a written message; earlier Trump had said any attempt to assassinate him would lead the U.S. to ‘completely decimate’ Iran

items.title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; target ₹35,000 crore trade by 2030
items.description: A joint statement noted that Narendra Modi and Christopher Luxon exchanged views on the Indo-Pacific, reaffirming their shared commitment to a free, open, and prosperous region

items.title: West Asia war updates: Iran warns it will not be bound by deal with U.S. if violations continue
items.description: Iran's supreme leader ​Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said ‌that ​avenging his ⁠slain predecessor and father was "the demand of ‌the nation" and "must certainly" take ‌place, according ‌to ⁠a written ⁠message released on his Telegram account

items.title: International penpals meet after 33-year correspondence
items.description: After three decades of letters, emails and social media exchanges, a chance encounter brings two penpals face-to-face for the first time.

items.title: SK hynix: From near collapse to AI chip powerhouse behind record US listing
items.description: Once on the edge of financial ruin, the South Korean chip giant has rebounded to debut on Wall Street in what is the largest US listing by a foreign company.

items.title: Vietnam: Indian tourists killed in boat capsize
items.description: The Indian embassy released the names of all 32 Indians who were aboard the boat that capsized near Phu Quoc island in Vietnam. At least 15 of them died.

items.title: Dating can be hard in South Korea
items.description: Dating can be hard in South Korea. A group of monks hope a 30-hour rural retreat can help.

items.title: Shein could aim for September or October IPO in Hong Kong, source says
Police in U.K. arrest suspect in former MP's murder
items.description: Widdecombe, 78, was found dead on July 9 in her Haytor Vale home on the edge of Dartmoor National Park in southwest England after sustaining what police said were ‘serious injuries’

items.title: Malian army retakes key northern town of Anefis after week-long battle
items.description: Mali's army has regained control of the key northern town of Anefis after fighting against rebel militants for nearly a week, Tuareg separatists and a military source said Friday.

items.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
items.description: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.
